title: Delhi High Court pulls up police over allegations of illegal detention, torture of activists
content: "You may be investigating a terrorist angle, but if you pick up someone, you have to follow the procedure," court said to Delhi Police

title: State may witness close contests in many constituencies
content: The presence of TVK founder Vijay is said to have turned the contest into a three-cornered one instead of the usual bipolar race, paving the way for closer electoral battles than in the past

title: How Visakhapatnam zoo keeps animals cool as temperatures rise
content: As peak summer sets in, Indira Gandhi Zoological Park steps up species-specific cooling and hydration measures to protect animals from heat stress and dehydration

title: Torn fisherfolk families in Thangachimadam turn to the polls as a last resort
content: Conviction that the ballots remained a vital tool for ‘freedom’ of fishmen families is what ultimately compell the villagers to head to the polling stations

title: The Karur stampede is yet to fade from the memory of the voters at Velusamypuram
content: Minutes past 2 p.m. Thursday, booth number 15 at the Panchayat Union Middle School, located a few yards away from the scene of the stampede, registered 81.31% polling, indicating that the tragedy has in no way dispirited the enthusiasm of the voters

title: MLC election: Congress, NCP SP coax Uddhav; BJP sends 30 names for five seats to central leadership
content: Both Congress and the NCP have stated that they will support Uddhav Thackeray if he decides to contest. However, Uddhav Thackeray has not yet confirmed his decision

title: West Bengal polls: Modi and Mamata spar over jhaal muri
content: After voting for the first phase of Assembly polls in West Bengal, the campaign has picked up for the final phase on April 29

title: Tamil Nadu elections 2026: Over 84% voter turnout in the State, highest in its electoral history
content: Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar said that it was the highest percentage of polling in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. “[The] ECI salutes each voter of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu,” he said

title: Bihar Government seeks ₹12,000 crore loan from RBI to cover delayed social security pensions
content: Tejashwi Yadav claimed the state government is bankrupt, but Deputy Chief Minister Vijay Kumar Choudhary dismissed this, saying it’s routine for the government to take loans for development

title: Stressing grave constitutional wrong, Lucknow lawyer pushes for release of Chinese national languishing in U.P. Jail after prison term
content: The Chinese national after completing his prison sentence is presently living inside a temporary detention centre established in the district jail with the permission of the District Magistrate

title: Prolonged electricity cuts in Punjab cities as mercury soars put AAP govt on defensive
content: PSPCL recently issued public notice announcing scheduled power outages from April 21 to 28 across major cities, citing system upgrade works aimed at improving electricity supply

title: West Bengal election 2026: Record turnout of over 92% in first phase amidst isolated incidents of violence
content: Before the 2026 elections, the highest turnout was recorded in 2011 when 84.33% voters exercised their franchise; the elections in 2011 saw Trinamool Congress defeating the 34-year Left Front government

title: UN General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock to visit India next week
content: During her visit to India, Ms. Baerbock will hold “multiple bilateral meetings with various government officials” and also meet the UN country team in India led by UN Resident Coordinator Stefan Priesner

title: YSRCP thriving on false narratives on NDA govt, says Minister Sandhya Rani
content: People are watching how the YSRCP leaders are behaving and will teach them another lesson in 2029, she says

title: Troubled waters: A.P., T.N. fishermen embroiled in a row
content: The seizure of four mechanised boats owned by Tamil Nadu fishermen at Juvvaladinne Fishing Harbour in Nellore district by local fisherfolk has prompted the Andhra Pradesh government to reinitiate boat patrolling across four coastal districts, bringing to the fore yet again a four-decade-old issue of illegal intrusion. The conflict takes a political turn and the repercussions are witnessed in 164 A.P. villages, narrates N. S. Chowdary

title: Domestic work and its quest for recognition
content: Despite Karnataka notifying the Code on Wages Rules under the broader Code on Wages, 2019 framework, aimed at standardising minimum wages, working hours and employer responsibilities, and a draft Domestic Workers (Social Security and Welfare) Bill, domestic workers remain outside the formal workforce, writes Rishita Khanna.

title: ‘No difference between Kauravas and Congress members’: M.P. CM on women’s reservation row
content: Mr. Yadav slammed the Opposition party by alleging that it had “thwarted” Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s plans to empower women

title: BJP claiming ‘fake credit’ for raising land acquisition compensation for farmers; law made by UPA govt, says Congress
content: The Madhya Pradesh Cabinet had approved an increase in the multiplication factor applicable to land acquisition in rural areas from 1.0 to 2.0. to provide farmers with compensation up to four times the market price

title: What Telangana’s survey shows about caste inequality
content: When caste is measured, the gap is not incremental but exponential, with SC and ST households structurally locked in despite economic growth

title: Telangana RTC strike | A transport system stuck in breakdown mode
content: Six years after a prolonged shutdown in 2019, an indefinite strike by Telangana State Road Transport Corporation employees has once again brought the State’s public transport system to a halt, leaving nearly 65 lakh commuters stranded. Unresolved demands, especially the absorption of 40,000 employees into government service, and a widening trust deficit between workers and the government have resurfaced. While the government has shown willingness for talks while terming the strike illegal, deeper structural issues such as an ageing fleet and rising operational strain continue to plague the corporation, reports Syed Mohammed

title: Pahalgam, a year on: Resilience falters, tourism struggles
content: After the initial shock and grief of the April 2025 terrorist attack that killed 26 unarmed men on south Kashmir’s Baisaran meadow, Peerzada Ashiq finds that locals grapple with an uncertain future. Tourism is low, livelihoods have been lost, and heightened security measures are in place

title: 19-year-old worker kills newborn minutes after delivery in factory toilet in Bengaluru
content: According to the police, the woman delivered a premature male baby after a seven‑month pregnancy and allegedly slit the newborn’s throat and disposed of the body

title: Congress questions EC notice to Kharge, alleges ulterior motives
content: The party objects to 24-hour deadline given to Mallikarjun Kharge to respond, describing it as insufficient given his ongoing election campaign engagements

title: A season to try and understand the people’s pulse
content: While journalists cannot ignore digital platforms, only field reporting offers a clear understanding of the public sentiment

title: Recent hits give Malayalam film industry a shot in the arm
content: A couple of young and talented filmmakers have scripted success in the Malayalam film industry through their recent ventures. G. Krishnakumar finds out how the youngsters have rekindled hope in the industry that has been struggling to stay afloat

title: Kerala fireworks tragedy: body parts found as search intensifies
content: Cadaver dogs aid recovery of body parts; over 300 kg of unexploded explosives collected as probe deepens

title: NOTA loses ground as electors in Tamil Nadu lean towards new political alternatives
content: In 2013, the Supreme Court directed the Election Commission of India to include NOTA as an option on electronic voting machines, allowing voters to formally reject all candidates

title: Lisa Gill appointed Chief Justice of A.P. High Court
content: She will assume office on April 25 consequent upon the retirement of current Chief Justice Dhiraj Singh Thakur on April 24

title: The real equity gap in higher education
content: Equity in higher education institutions is more constrained by gaps in representation in employment than in admissions; the UGC regulations focus largely on anti-discrimination and complaint-resolution mechanisms, but do not adequately address these structural gaps

title: Record turnout in WB & Tamil Nadu polls despite violence
content: Record voter turnout marked Phase 1 of the 2026 Assembly elections in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, despite sporadic violence and polling disruptions.

title: Residents near Katpadi boycott polls after relocation of polling booth
content: Citing relocation of the booth to a farther distance from the colony and a lack of civic amenities in the area, residents chose not to vote

title: Polling booth agent arrested for damaging EVM in Tiruvannamalai
content: The damaged EVM was replaced with another machine to continue polling, which was delayed by around 15 minutes

title: France waives airport transit visa requirement for Indians
content: The move follows French President Emmanuel Macron’s announcement during his visit to India in February 2026 that he would take measures to ease travel for Indians via France

title: Pandalam family member criticises Kerala for stance in Sabarimala entry dispute
content: Senior representative of erstwhile royal family criticises government in Supreme Court for not being firm in opposing petitions filed by non-believers questioning the age-old rituals of Sabarimala temple

title: Over 88 per cent polling in Vellore, nearby districts
content: Election officials said that among four districts, Tiruvannamalai registered the highest polling percentage with 88.98%, followed by Tirupattur (88.62%), Vellore (88.61%), and Ranipet (88.04%) as on 8.30 p.m on Thursday

title: Mojtaba Khamenei awaiting prosthetic leg after strike, suffered severe facial burns
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei faces significant medical challenges post-bomb attack, impacting his direct engagement. This has amplified the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' influence, with analysts suggesting a shift towards a 'board-style' leadership where military commanders hold sway.  The situation casts uncertainty over ongoing ceasefire talks and diplomatic pathways.

title: IPL 2026: Why concussion sub for a shoulder injury? Mahela Jayawardene explains
content: Mumbai Indians faced controversy in their IPL 2026 loss to Chennai Super Kings after using Shardul Thakur as a concussion substitute for Mitchell Santner. Coach Mahela Jayawardene defended the decision, citing a head injury, though Santner later wore a shoulder sling. The debate overshadowed MI's continued struggles on the field.

title: ‘Felt humiliated after sacking’: Accused had ill intentions towards IRS officer’s daughter
content: A 19-year-old man, Rahul Meena, is accused of a brutal rape and murder of an IRS officer's daughter in Delhi, allegedly committing another sexual assault in Alwar hours prior. Familiar with the victim's home, he used a spare key, attacked her, and stole valuables. He confessed to the crime, citing money as motive.

title: Meta tells staff it will lay off 8,000 on May 20 as plan had already leaked
content: Meta is preparing to lay off approximately 10% of its global workforce, impacting around 8,000 employees on May 20. This decision, announced by Chief People Officer Janelle Gale, aims to increase efficiency and offset significant AI investments. The company will provide generous severance packages and career support to affected employees.

title: Bengal polls: Empty polling booths, scarred villages at ground zero of SIR rage
content: Mothabari voters, many with EPIC cards, were turned away on polling day, their names deleted from electoral rolls despite prior voting and document submissions.  Migrant workers returned hoping to rectify their status, only to find their names still missing. This exclusion cast a shadow over the democratic process, leaving many feeling disenfranchised and questioning the fairness of the system.

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bharat Electronics, and Colgate-Palmolive (India) have been recommended as the top stocks to buy today (April 24, 2026) by Bajaj Broking Research. Take a look at the target prices and expected returns:

title: PSL drama! Foreign coach loses cool, starts swearing in presser - Watch
content: Islamabad United coach Luke Ronchi lost his temper during a PSL 2026 press conference after his team’s six-wicket defeat to RawalPindiz. Interrupted mid-answer, he snapped, "He can ask me a question, I don't give a s**t." RawalPindiz secured their first win after eight losses, while Islamabad United remain in playoff contention with two matches left.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: Oil prices today: Crude climbs as US-Iran talks uncertainty keeps markets on edge
content: Middle East tensions surged Friday, pushing oil prices up as Iran released footage of commandos boarding a ship in the Strait of Hormuz and reported military activity over Tehran. Analysts warn prolonged disruption could severely impact global supply, with inventories potentially falling to multi-year lows.  Markets remain on edge as diplomatic efforts face uncertainty.

title: US soldier used secret Maduro raid intel to win $400k betting jackpot, Trump reacts
content: A US Special Forces soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, has been charged with exploiting classified intelligence from a covert mission to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro. He allegedly used this sensitive information to place bets on an online prediction market, Polymarket, profiting over $400,000.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' falls short of Rs 20 crore at end of week 5; total collections hit Rs 1,766 crore
content: Blockbuster 'Dhurandhar 2' is experiencing a slowdown, hitting its lowest collection of Rs 1.40 crore net on Thursday, its 36th day. After a phenomenal opening and crossing Rs 674 crore in its first week, the film's collections have steadily declined. Despite this, its worldwide gross collection is an impressive Rs 1,766.06 crore.

title: IRGC warns it may cut undersea internet cables in Persian Gulf: What it may mean
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has warned that undersea data cables in the Strait of Hormuz are highly vulnerable, potentially causing severe internet outages across the Persian Gulf. These vital digital arteries carry a significant portion of regional internet traffic and support major AI infrastructure, impacting global communication, finance, and cloud services.

title: Russini’s husband faces scrutiny over claims he knew of alleged Vrabel affair
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ reporter and NFL insider, Dianna Russini, continue to dominate headlines as their alleged affair rumors swirl. As Dianna Russini has stepped down from her role at The Athletic, new allegations have been made about her marital life that she has always tried to keep out of the spotlight.

title: Final Instagram post of Brazilian triathlete Mara Araujo resurfaces after death
content: Brazilian triathlete Mara Flavia Araujo died during the swimming leg of the Ironman Texas triathlon on April 19, 2026. Her last Instagram post, shared hours earlier, has gone viral after her death. Reports from Chosun.com and Yahoo confirm she went missing during the swim. A close friend revealed she was unwell before the race but still chose to compete.

title: 'He fathered her child': Claims about Russini’s son spark new Vrabel saga twist
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has publicly announced that he is taking some time off to seek counselling as the alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini refuse to die down as new details emerge. Amid all the mess, it seems the situation has taken a turn for the worse as Dianna Russini’s husband and child have been dragged into the drama.

title: Heated on-field moment! Tilak Varma, Jamie Overton clash as SKY steps in – Watch
content: A heated on-field exchange between MI's Tilak Varma and CSK's Jamie Overton, with Suryakumar Yadav intervening, marked the IPL clash between Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings. Despite the mid-game drama, the players reconciled post-match. However, CSK dominated the game, securing a commanding 103-run victory powered by Sanju Samson's century.

title: 1 killed, 5 injured in shooting at Mall of Louisiana food court
content: A shooting at the Mall of Louisiana in Baton Rouge has claimed one life and injured five. The incident occurred in the food court following a confrontation between two groups. Police have taken five individuals into custody and confirmed there is no ongoing threat. Shoppers and employees scrambled for safety during the chaos.

title: From mother's last rites to CSK's thumping win: Mukesh Choudhary bowls through pain
content: Mukesh Choudhary displayed immense courage. He played for his team Chennai Super Kings just a day after his mother's passing. He performed her last rites and then rejoined the squad. Choudhary took a wicket in his first over. He dedicated this moment to his mother. Chennai Super Kings secured a dominant victory over Mumbai Indians.

title: Delhi sees hottest day of the year; heatwave warning for today and tomorrow
content: Delhi sweltered through its hottest day of the year, with temperatures soaring to 43 degrees Celsius. The Met department forecasts continued heatwaves for Friday and Saturday, urging residents to stay hydrated. Air quality saw improvement, moving to moderate, though authorities are cracking down on road dust and construction waste violations.

title: Small towns' big push: Sitapur's Shikha Verma and Kashish Verma top UP boards
content: Shikha Verma and Kashish Verma from Sitapur have topped the Class XII and X UP Board exams respectively, with girls once again outperforming boys. The results highlight the growing academic strength of smaller districts, with students from these areas dominating the top ranks. Reforms aimed at ensuring transparent and copy-free examinations are credited for the smooth conduct and improved results.

title: IPL: Ishan Kishan, Abhishek Sharma turn 'secret choreographers' for Travis Head - Watch
content: During a Sunrisers Hyderabad team photoshoot, captain Ishan Kishan and opener Abhishek Sharma hilariously guided Australian star Travis Head through dance steps. This light-hearted moment reflects the team's strong camaraderie off the field, complementing their solid IPL 2026 performance. SRH currently sits fourth in the standings, with Abhishek Sharma leading the run charts.

title: Titans linked to blockbuster trade for Falcons Star Kyle Pitts amid 2026 draft
content: The Tennessee Titans experienced a disastrous run on the field in the last few seasons as they recorded just 3 wins and 14 losses in both 2025 and 2024, sparking major concern among their fans in the league. It seems the team failed to show any improvement in their performance even as the Tennessee Titans focused on a roster change.

title: Thanks SIR, Tamil Nadu records 85% polling
content: Polling this time was the highest in the state’s electoral history, with 4.85 crore of the 5.73 crore on the electoral rolls casting their votes.

title: Vrabel under fire for allegedly mocking wife’s athleticism in Russini interview
content: ​Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affair rumors are only getting worse by the minute as new photos from their night out in 2020 surface sparking chaos. While Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini were spotted sharing a kiss at a bar in 2020, a new clip of them making controversial remarks about the New England Patriots’ head coach’s wife has sparked outrage.

title: Anupam Kher chose renting over owning after selling home to Alia
content: Veteran actor Anupam Kher has never shied away from speaking about his humble beginnings, and his recent revelations about selling his Mumbai apartment reflect the same philosophy. The actor, who once sold a property later purchased by Alia Bhatt, has now opened up about why he consciously chose not to own a house at one point in his life.

title: How Karnataka Bypolls Could Decide Crisis Manager DK Shivakumar's Future
content: The bypolls in Bagalkot and Davanagere South were not just local contests, but a test of how united the Karnataka Congress really is.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says US "In No Rush" To End Iran War
content: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: The standoff between the US and Iran has effectively choked off nearly all exports through the strait -- where 20 per cent of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime -- with no end in sight.

content: Trump said he has no plans of deploying nuclear arms but maintained that the Islamic Republic was already "decimated" through conventional means.

title: Heatwave May Melt 4% Of GDP, Raise Medical Bills, Dent Productivity
content: Extreme heat has already accounted for around $159 billion in lost productivity, roughly 5.4 per cent of India's income.

content: In Washington, at least 11 incidents involving scientists in areas such as nuclear technology, space research and advanced weapons are under scrutiny.

title: Millions Of Americans May Now Also Be Considered Canadian Under New Law
content: Since the new law took effect December 15, immigration lawyers in the United States and Canada say they have been overwhelmed by clients seeking help submitting proof of citizenship applications.

title: Delhi Murder Autopsy Reveals Ex-Help's Brutality, Woman's Fight To Save Life
content: The woman was raped and murdered on Wednesday morning in Delhi, allegedly by her family's former domestic help.

title: Teen Killed Over Old Instagram Post In Ahmedabad, 3 Arrested
content: The three accused allegedly stabbed the victim Mohammed Salik Imtiyaz Hussain Sheikh to death on Wednesday night.

title: Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks
content: With Trump struggling to push Iran into a deal on the wider regional war, the United States is hoping for progress on Lebanon, which Israel has pounded in response to Hezbollah fire.

content: The president and his cabinet have largely been tasked with maintaining internal stability, ensuring consistent supplies of essentials like food and fuel, and keeping day-to-day governance running smoothly, according to officials familiar with the si

content: Meta will lay off about 8,000 employees and leave thousands of other positions next month, a source told AFP.

content: the bench of justices raised questions over how HC dealt with the case . HC dismissed the appeal as non-maintainable, but re-examined the case after SC disagreed with this view . high court set aside the trial court's acquittal order .

title: Government says no plan to hike fuel prices despite OMCs incurring losses
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. Read More

title: Judiciary can’t interfere in devotees’ sacrosanct right to manner of worship, says Supreme Court
content: the supreme court agreed that devotees’ right to decide the mode and manner of worshipping God was non-justiciable . the remark came after senior advocate J Muth Raj’s argument that in Hinduism, every village had a ‘gram devta’ and every family had ‘ishta devtes’ . it was impossible for the court to examine the essentiality of such practices while tracing it to the umbrella religion .

title: Congress leader moves SC alleging PM Modi’s DD speech violated MCC
content:
.

NEW DELHI: Congress functionary TN Prathapan has approached the Supreme Court, alleging violation of the model code of conduct due to the broadcast of PM Modi’s April 18 speech by state-run channels Doordarshan and Sansad TV. The petition, which lies in defect as per its status on the SC website, claimed the PM’s speech, blaming opposition parties for the defeat of the 131st Constitutional amendment bill, was used to influence voters during the ongoing elections. It is alleged that the use of publicly funded broadcasters to air a political speech amounted to the misuse of official machinery. The plea said this fell within the definition of corrupt practice under section 123(7) of Representation of People Act, which barred the use of government machinery for electoral purposes. It noted that a complaint was also submitted to EC.

End of Article

title: 'There's reason why women offer namaz at home,' says SC judge
content: justice amanullah said there was no ban on women offering namaz in mosques since the days of the Prophet . there were customs and procedures in Islam regulating how can they offer prayer . the nine-judge bench must overrule the 1994 SC judgment in Ismail Farooqui case .

title: HC slams EC for 'abusing power' to put curbs on bikes
content: EC imposed a 48-hour restriction on motorbikes in Kolkata and parganas from 6pm on April 27 . he criticised EC for "abusing its power" and called the restriction a 'drastic move' the court's scepticism was hailed as a victory for the common citizen .

title: Hackers steal $2.5m from Lankan finance ministry
content: the cyberattack is a major blow to Sri Lanka, which is recovering from a crippling economic crisis in 2022 . the money was destined as debt repayment to Australia, finance ministry secretary Harshana Suriyapperuma said . four senior officers at the public debt management office (PDMO) were suspended after the breach .

title: West Bengal SIR: Nandalal Bose’s 88-yr-old grandson made to try thrice before voting despite SC relief
content: the family of the grandson of painter's grandson voted in Santiniketan . a tribunal cleared their names after the matter was escalated to the DM . the couple were escorted to the booth around 4pm to vote .

title: Not abandoned by government during 2020 LAC standoff, says Naravane after book row
content: MM Naravane has written a new book, 'the Curious and the Classified: Unearthing Military Myths and Mysteries' he said he had the government’s full backing and authority to open fire on Chinese soldiers if the situation so warranted during the LAC standoff with China .

title: Indian Navy lists Hormuz as ‘primary area’ of interest, one of the key ‘choke points’
content: Maritime choke points in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) constitute critical nodes that exert disproportionate influence over global trade and energy security . such choke points are Cape of Good Hope, Mozambique Channel, Bab-el-Mandeb, Suez Canal, Strait of Hormuz, Malacca and Singapore Straits, Sunda Strait, Lombok Strait and Ombai Strait .
